terms.definition: Linear regression trained by minimizing mean squared error on labeled data — the standard closed-form and gradient-based fit for ŷ = Xw + b. Canonical treatment in this topic.
Learned coefficients multiplying each feature in ŷ = wᵀx + b; in matrix form, w aligns with columns of X.
Scalar baseline added to the weighted sum of features; often absorbed as an extra weight on a constant feature column.
n × d matrix whose rows are training examples and columns are features (plus optional column of ones for bias).
Closed-form OLS solution w* = (XᵀX)⁻¹Xᵀy derived from setting the MSE gradient to zero. Canonical intuition in this topic.
Prediction error on one example: yᵢ − ŷᵢ; OLS minimizes the sum of squared residuals.
